title: AB 2053, the California Social Housing Act, passed out of the Assembly and is now headed to the Senate. AB 2053, by Assemblymember Alex Lee (D-San José) establishes Social Housing

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 2053 establishes Social Housing in California by creating the California Housing Authority (CHA) to produce and preserve mixed-income housing.",
    "Social Housing aims to remove the profit motive from building affordable housing.",
    "California has been losing lower and middle-income residents due to the high cost of living.",
    "Over 97% of cities and counties in California have been unable to produce enough affordable housing.",
    "There are only enough affordable and available rentals for 24% of extremely low income households in California.",
    "The approval of the State Assembly signals a paradigm shift in viewing housing not as a commodity, but as a human right.",
    "Social Housing will develop mixed-income projects in desirable locations close to transit, parks, and recreation.",
    "Social Housing is financially self-sustaining, with higher income residents subsidizing low-income units.",
    "The goal of Social Housing is for tenants to spend no more than 30% of their income on rent.",
    "Mixed-income housing models have been successfully implemented in locations such as Sacramento, Montgomery County, Vienna, and Singapore."
  ]
}
```
